lesson.menu_title: Scheduling a Meeting -- Planning When to Meet
lesson.menu_desc: Dialogue + prepositions of time (on / at / in) and making plans -- 30 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 7 -- Scheduling a Meeting -- Planning When to Meet
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maísa de Oliveira Santos | Aula 7 -- Scheduling a Meeting

listenings.text: Hi Maisa, this is David. I have a small problem with our meeting. I am not available on Monday morning. I have an appointment at nine. Can we reschedule? I am free on Tuesday afternoon, at three PM. Please confirm the new time by email. Also, I want to book the big meeting room for our call. Thank you. See you on Tuesday!

Hello, this is Sarah from the finance team. I would like to invite you to a call next week. Are you free on Wednesday? We usually start at ten in the morning. The call is about the new client. It is not long, only thirty minutes. Please confirm your schedule with me. I am available every day this week, except Friday. I look forward to our call!

extra_audio.text: Hello David. Are you free on Monday? Can we meet at three in the afternoon? Let us confirm the meeting by email.
